commit.message:
fix: reorder klinecharts v10 init to match official pattern

setDataLoader must be called AFTER setSymbol/setPeriod per the
klinecharts v10 docs. Previously it was set once during init (before
any data existed) and never re-registered when data arrived, so
getBars was never triggered. Now follows the official pattern:
setSymbol → setPeriod → setDataLoader on every data change.

fix: make AI credits balance visible without BLS auth

The balance endpoint was requiring a full BLS challenge-sign-verify flow
just to view a credit count. This had ~6 failure modes (WalletConnect
session_delete race, Sage field name changes, timing issues) causing
credits to always show 0.

Architecture change: balance endpoint is now public (wallet address via
query param, like Phase 2 credits), and BLS auth is deferred to when
the user actually tries to spend credits (enhance action). This matches
the Phase 2 credits pattern which works perfectly.

- /api/ai/balance: accept ?wallet=xch1... instead of requireAuth
- AIEnhanceContext: fetch balance on wallet connect (no session needed)
- Lazy auth: BLS signing only triggered on first enhance attempt
- ensureAuthenticated: tries localStorage cache before prompting sign
- Fix bls12-381 import for Cloudflare Pages bundler

Then dispatch a window resize event so ALL responsive charts in the
flex layout recalculate their dimensions properly.

* Fix flex layout distortion: zero-width element before relayout

After moving chart back into flex container, temporarily set width:0
and min-width:0 so the element doesn't influence flex space distribution.
Then on next animation frame, clear those styles and let Plotly relayout
to the correct container width. This prevents the residual fullscreen
pixel width from distorting sibling flex items.

* Fix fullscreen exit: purge and recreate chart from saved data

Instead of trying to reset Plotly's internal state, save the original
data/layout before fullscreen, then on exit use Plotly.purge + newPlot
to completely rebuild the chart from scratch. This guarantees no
residual fullscreen dimensions affect the flex layout.

* Fix fullscreen: placeholder preserves flex space + resize siblings

Root cause: when chart is removed from flex container for fullscreen,
the sibling chart expands to fill all space. When chart returns, the
sibling stays expanded.

2. After recreating the chart, resize all sibling Plotly charts in

commit.message:
chore: replace python-jose with PyJWT, update all deps to latest stable

## Dependency changes

### Replaced
- python-jose[cryptography] 3.5.0 → PyJWT 2.11.0

  python-jose is no longer actively maintained and has outstanding CVEs.
  PyJWT is the de-facto standard, actively maintained, and has an identical
  jwt.decode() call signature. Exception handling updated from JWTError to
  jwt.exceptions.InvalidTokenError (base class covering DecodeError,
  ExpiredSignatureError, InvalidSignatureError, etc.).

### Removed
- passlib[bcrypt] — was only used in security.py (dead code since the
  email/password auth refactor). No production code imports it.
- bcrypt — same reason as above.

### Updated (production)
- fastapi          0.121.3 → 0.135.1
- granian          2.6.0   → 2.7.2
- pydantic         2.12.4  → 2.12.5
- pydantic-settings 2.12.0 → 2.13.1
- python-multipart 0.0.20  → 0.0.22
- uvicorn          0.38.0  → 0.41.0

  (motor 3.7.1, beanie 2.0.0, apscheduler 3.11.2, tenacity 9.1.4,
  httpx 0.28.1, asgi-lifespan 2.1.0 are already at latest stable)

### Updated (dev)
- pytest           7.4.0   → 9.0.2
- pytest-asyncio   0.21.0  → 1.3.0
- ruff             0.1.0   → 0.15.5
- pytest-cov       4.0.0   → 7.0.0

  (respx 0.22.0, pytest-mock 3.15.1, mongomock-motor 0.0.36 are at latest)

## Code changes

### app/core/auth.py
- `from jose import JWTError, jwt` → `import jwt`
- `except JWTError:` → `except jwt.exceptions.InvalidTokenError:`

### app/core/security.py
- Replaced dead code (bcrypt + python-jose) with a stub comment.
  This file is not imported anywhere in production code.

### New
- **GitHub Code Review integration** — new "Get automatic code review on every PR" row in the overview table linking to `/en/code-review` (getting-started-doc)
- **`CronCreate`, `CronDelete`, `CronList` tools** — session-scoped scheduled/one-shot prompts; documented in the tools table with links to `/en/scheduled-tasks` (settings-doc)
- **`EnterWorktree` / `ExitWorktree` tools** — create and leave isolated git worktrees from within a session (settings-doc)
- **`EnterPlanMode` tool** — switches to plan mode to design an approach before coding (settings-doc)
- **`ListMcpResourcesTool` / `ReadMcpResourceTool` tools** — list and read MCP server resources (settings-doc)
- **`TaskStop` tool** — kills a running background task by ID (settings-doc)
- **`TodoWrite` tool** — manages the session task checklist in non-interactive mode and the Agent SDK (settings-doc)
- **`ToolSearch` tool** — renamed from `MCPSearch`; searches for and loads deferred tools (settings-doc)
- **VS Code `vscode://anthropic.claude-code/open` URI handler** — opens a new Claude Code tab programmatically with optional `prompt` and `session` query parameters (operations-doc)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: pivot to operator-managed Discord access model (Telegram parity)

## Overview

Complete architectural pivot from a self-service SaaS model (users register,
pay, get access via Stripe webhooks) to an operator-managed access model where
admins grant/revoke Discord guild memberships on behalf of external payment
systems. This mirrors the ra-tg-service (Telegram) pattern exactly, adapted
to Discord's guild/role mechanics.

The external API contract is now: operator holds a JWT, calls grant-access
with an ext_user_id, and gets back either instant activation or a signed
OAuth2 URL to send the user.

---

## Breaking Changes

### Removed
- `User` document (email, hashed_password, is_admin) — replaced by JWT-only auth
- `Subscription` document — replaced by `DiscordMembership`
- `SubscriptionTier` document — tiers replaced by inline role_ids per grant
- `SubscriptionStatus` enum — replaced by `MembershipStatus`
- `app/core/security.py` (bcrypt hashing, JWT creation) — tokens issued externally
- `POST /auth/register` — no self-service accounts
- `POST /auth/login` — no password auth
- `GET /auth/discord/login` — OAuth now initiated via grant-access signed URL
- All `/users/*` endpoints — no self-service user management
- All `/admin/*` endpoints — replaced by new `/discord/*` router
- `POST /webhooks/external-payment` — payment webhooks no longer handled here
- `WEBHOOK_SECRET` config var — removed
- `JWT_ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES` config var — tokens issued externally, no expiry managed here

### Changed
- `app/core/auth.py` — rewritten to JWT-only; checks Bearer header, x-access-token
  header, then cookie; requires `username` claim (owner_id); no admin flag
- `app/core/config.py` — removed WEBHOOK_SECRET + JWT_ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES;
  added DISCORD_STATE_SECRET (HMAC signing) and DISCORD_OAUTH_SUCCESS_URL
- `app/database/database.py` — updated Beanie model registration to new documents
- `app/core/scheduler.py` — expired_membership_sweeper rewritten for DiscordMembership;
  discord_state_reconciler updated to use DiscordExternalUser + ensure_valid_token()
- `app/database/models.py` — complete rewrite (see New Models below)
- `app/api/endpoints/auth.py` — rewritten; only Discord OAuth2 callback remains;
  now verifies HMAC state, links discord_id to ext_user_id, activates memberships
- `app/main.py` — updated router registration; removed old routers; added discord_access
- `app/api/endpoints/admin.py`, `users.py`, `webhooks.py` — emptied (dead code stubs)

---

## New Models (app/database/models.py)

### DiscordExternalUser
Primary record for external users, keyed by ext_user_id from the payment system.
discord_id is populated only after the user completes the OAuth2 linking flow.

  Collection: discord_external_users
  Indexes:
    - unique on ext_user_id
    - unique sparse on discord_id (only indexed when non-null)

Fields: ext_user_id, discord_id, discord_access_token, discord_refresh_token,
        discord_token_expires_at, created_at, updated_at

### DiscordMembership
One record per (ext_user_id, guild_id) pair. Re-granting extends the period
rather than creating a duplicate — enforced by compound unique index.

  Collection: discord_memberships
  Indexes:
    - compound unique on (ext_user_id, guild_id)
    - (status, current_period_end) — used by expiry sweeper
    - (guild_id, current_period_end) — used for per-guild queries
    - ext_user_id — all memberships for a user

Fields: ext_user_id, guild_id, role_ids[], status (MembershipStatus),
        current_period_end, owner_id, ref, created_at, updated_at

### MembershipStatus enum
  active | pending_oauth | expired | cancelled

### AuditLog extensions
Added ext_user_id and ref fields to AuditLog for cross-service tracing.

### New AuditAction values
  ACCESS_GRANTED, ACCESS_REVOKED, FORCE_REMOVED, OAUTH_LINKED, EXPIRE_MEMBERSHIP

### New request/response schemas
  RegisterGuildRequest, GrantAccessRequest, GrantAccessResponse,
  ForceRemoveRequest, SendMessageRequest, MembershipResponse

---

## New Files

### app/utils/oauth_state.py
HMAC-SHA256 signed OAuth2 state parameter utilities.

The state encodes {ext_user_id, guild_ids, ref} directly in the URL,
signed with DISCORD_STATE_SECRET. No database write needed before redirect —
context is carried entirely in the URL.

  Format: <base64url_payload>.<hex_hmac_sha256_signature>

  create_signed_state(ext_user_id, guild_ids, ref) -> str
  verify_signed_state(state) -> dict  (raises ValueError if tampered)

Uses hmac.compare_digest for constant-time comparison to prevent timing attacks.

### app/api/endpoints/discord_access.py
New core router — all 6 operator endpoints:

  POST /api/v1/discord/guilds
    Register a Discord guild. Validates bot presence via GET /guilds/{id}.
    Upserts (safe to re-register). Stores owner_id from JWT username.

  GET /api/v1/discord/guilds
    List guilds owned by the authenticated user. Includes active member count.

  GET /api/v1/discord/guilds/{guild_id}/members
    List memberships in a guild. Supports ?status= and ?period_end_before= filters.
    Joins DiscordExternalUser to include discord_id in response.
    403 if guild not owned by token holder.

  POST /api/v1/discord/grant-access
    Core endpoint. Upserts DiscordExternalUser + DiscordMembership.
    period_end = now + period_days, set to 23:59:59 UTC on final day.
    If discord_id known: immediate activation (refresh token, add to guild, assign roles).
    If discord_id unknown: returns signed oauth_url, status=pending_oauth.
    Re-calling extends the period (idempotent). Reactivates expired/cancelled memberships.

  POST /api/v1/discord/force-remove
    Removes all role_ids from member, kicks from guild, sets status=cancelled.
    Supports dry_run=true to simulate without side effects.
    Writes FORCE_REMOVED audit log entry.

  POST /api/v1/discord/send-message
    Posts a message to a channel via the bot. Guild ownership verified.

---

## Modified Files

### app/services/discord_service.py
Added two methods:

  get_guild(guild_id) -> dict | None
    Bot-level guild info fetch. Used by register_guild to validate bot presence.
    Returns None if bot is not in the guild (404).

  ensure_valid_token(user: DiscordExternalUser) -> str
    Auto-refreshes the user's Discord OAuth access token if it expires within
    5 minutes. Updates DiscordExternalUser in DB when refreshed.
    Used by grant-access (immediate path) and discord_state_reconciler.

### app/core/scheduler.py — expired_membership_sweeper rewrite
Old logic: Subscription → SubscriptionTier → DiscordServer → User
New logic: DiscordMembership → DiscordExternalUser

Key behavior changes:
- Marks status=expired BEFORE Discord API calls (idempotent — concurrent runs safe)
- pending_oauth memberships expire silently (no discord_id, nothing to revoke)
- Kick only if no other active memberships remain for (ext_user_id, guild_id)
- Writes EXPIRE_MEMBERSHIP audit log per membership processed

### app/core/scheduler.py — discord_state_reconciler update
Updated from User/Subscription/SubscriptionTier chain to
DiscordExternalUser/DiscordMembership. Now calls ensure_valid_token() before
re-adding users to the guild (handles expired OAuth tokens transparently).

### app/api/endpoints/auth.py — OAuth callback rewrite
Old: Required authenticated user, linked Discord to their account.
New: Public endpoint. Verifies HMAC state, exchanges code, upserts
DiscordExternalUser, activates all pending_oauth memberships, redirects to
DISCORD_OAUTH_SUCCESS_URL.

---

## OAuth2 Linking Flow

First-time grant-access:
  1. Operator calls POST /discord/grant-access {ext_user_id, guild_ids, ...}
  2. DiscordExternalUser created (no discord_id)
  3. DiscordMembership created (status: pending_oauth)
  4. Signed state = HMAC-SHA256(DISCORD_STATE_SECRET, base64url({ext_user_id, guild_ids, ref}))
  5. oauth_url returned with signed state embedded

User visits oauth_url once:
  6. Discord redirects to GET /auth/discord/callback?code=...&state=...
  7. State signature verified (constant-time)
  8. Code exchanged for OAuth tokens
  9. discord_id fetched from GET /users/@me
  10. DiscordExternalUser upserted with discord_id + tokens
  11. All pending_oauth memberships activated (add_member_to_guild + assign roles)
  12. Redirect to DISCORD_OAUTH_SUCCESS_URL

Subsequent grant-access (discord_id already known):
  - ensure_valid_token() refreshes OAuth token if needed
  - Immediate add_member_to_guild() + add_role_to_member()
  - Returns {status: active, oauth_url: null}

---

## Documentation

### README.md
Complete rewrite: operator model overview, OAuth linking flow diagrams,
updated env var table, new endpoint overview, updated project structure.

### docs/api.md
Complete rewrite: removed all register/login/users/admin/webhooks sections.
Full reference for all 6 new endpoints with request bodies, response shapes,
error tables, and end-to-end cURL workflow.

### docs/architecture.md
Complete rewrite: updated entity relationship diagram, new data model docs,
JWT auth pattern section (external tokens, username claim), OAuth2 state flow
section with HMAC explanation, ensure_valid_token() documentation.

### docs/background-jobs.md
Rewritten sweeper section for DiscordMembership model. Added pending_oauth
expiry explanation. Updated reconciler section for DiscordExternalUser +
ensure_valid_token(). Updated monitoring log message table.

### .env.example
Removed JWT_ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES + WEBHOOK_SECRET.
Added DISCORD_STATE_SECRET + DISCORD_OAUTH_SUCCESS_URL with usage comments.
Updated JWT comment to explain external token pattern.

only disconnects if OUR session was actually deleted.
